# UC-22.6.50 · ISO/IEC 27001:2022 Clause 9.2 — Internal audit coverage: control sample rotation

## Description

Joins the ISMS control catalogue with internal-audit test records over the trailing 365 days. The output tells the Audit Committee which controls are overdue.

## Value

Closes the typical gap where internal audit over-samples 'hot' controls and silently skips administrative ones. The rotation evidence is what ISO 9.2 requires and what the registrar looks for.

## Implementation

(1) Maintain iso_27001_control_catalog.csv; (2) require internal-audit output to index=grc with control_id tag; (3) schedule UC weekly; (4) due_for_audit=1 adds the control to the next quarter audit plan; (5) annual Audit Committee report. Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=archer:audit_test`, `sourcetype=servicenow:audit_finding`, `sourcetype=servicenow:audit_finding;`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=grc` sourcetype=archer:audit_test OR sourcetype=servicenow:audit_finding; ISMS control catalogue `iso_27001_control_catalog.csv`..
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Maintain iso_27001_control_catalog.csv; (2) require internal-audit output to index=grc with control_id tag; (3) schedule UC weekly; (4) due_for_audit=1 adds the control to the next quarter audit plan; (5) annual Audit Committee report.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
| inputlookup iso_27001_control_catalog.csv
| join type=outer control_id
  [search index=grc sourcetype IN (archer:audit_test,servicenow:audit_finding) earliest=-365d
   | stats latest(_time) AS last_audited count AS audit_count_365d BY control_id]
| eval days_since=round((now()-last_audited)/86400,0)
| eval due_for_audit=if(isnull(last_audited) OR days_since>365,1,0)
| table control_id category last_audited days_since audit_count_365d due_for_audit
```

Understanding this SPL

**ISO/IEC 27001:2022 Clause 9.2 — Internal audit coverage: control sample rotation** — Closes the typical gap where internal audit over-samples 'hot' controls and silently skips administrative ones. The rotation evidence is what ISO 9.2 requires and what the registrar looks for.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=grc` sourcetype=archer:audit_test OR sourcetype=servicenow:audit_finding; ISMS control catalogue `iso_27001_control_catalog.csv`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Loads rows via `inputlookup` (KV store or CSV lookup) for enrichment or reporting.
• Correlates data from a subsearch to enrich the primary result set with additional context.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **days_since** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **due_for_audit** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.

Output fields: audit_count_365d, category, control_id, control_id], days_since, due_for_audit, last_audited.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with the GRC system, audit register, or framework crosswalk. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Stacked bar of audit count by category, table of overdue controls, single value 'controls never audited'.

## SPL

```spl
| inputlookup iso_27001_control_catalog.csv
| join type=outer control_id
  [search index=grc sourcetype IN (archer:audit_test,servicenow:audit_finding) earliest=-365d
   | stats latest(_time) AS last_audited count AS audit_count_365d BY control_id]
| eval days_since=round((now()-last_audited)/86400,0)
| eval due_for_audit=if(isnull(last_audited) OR days_since>365,1,0)
| table control_id category last_audited days_since audit_count_365d due_for_audit
```

## Visualization

Stacked bar of audit count by category, table of overdue controls, single value 'controls never audited'.
